title: CausalBench Perturb-seq Gene-Regulatory-Network Benchmark

data_urls.access_notes: Paper and source point to the public benchmark framework and source data.
Use for benchmark semantics, train regimes, and citation.

instructions: Use the official CausalBench repository to recover download/preprocessing commands and versioned benchmark splits.
Start with one cell line and one training regime before expanding to all benchmark settings.
Record source dataset, cell line, intervention target, guide/perturbation identity, expression representation, network-prior source, and split.

scale.download_size: large single-cell perturbation benchmark; reported real-world tasks exceed 200,000 training samples

recommended_axes.name: perturbation_target_support
recommended_axes.definition: Distance from training perturbation targets using gene family, pathway, protein interaction, or gene-embedding similarity.

recommended_axes.name: cell_line_support
recommended_axes.definition: Cell-line or cellular-context similarity, including K562/RPE1 transfer when both are used.

recommended_axes.name: network_neighborhood_support
recommended_axes.definition: Similarity of candidate edges or target genes in known interaction/regulatory-prior neighborhoods.

recommended_axes.name: expression_state_support
recommended_axes.definition: Nearest-neighbor support in basal or perturbed expression space.

useful_for: Testing whether a single-cell or causal-discovery model generalizes to unseen perturbation targets.
Auditing whether GRN-inference performance depends on prior-network support.
Building spectral curves over perturbation, cell-line, and biological-network novelty.
leakage_risks: Guide identity, perturbation target, and target family can leak across splits.
Cell line can become a split label rather than a biological novelty axis.
External prior networks may overlap with labels or evaluation edges.

paperclip_provenance.notes: Paperclip repeatedly surfaced CausalBench as a real-world single-cell perturbation benchmark for network inference; official repository and paper confirm public benchmark code and source-data links.
